## [03:03 - 06:05]

Now I discussed it on the next call in my Facebook ads coach because as I said, you know what happened? Zero people showed up. Not a single person. Like out of these, whatever like 400, not even one. And I was so, so, so confused. So here's basically the debrief from it guys. First of all, the ad that I was running was like, because they've been around 80 years and they're literally London's most expensive, most prestigious sort of, they're a garage and a car wash and they also do super high end cars and stuff like that. You know, the cars that go through there are literally Bugatti's, Ferrari's. Like, in my time there, I very rarely saw a car go in there. There was less than minimum and had to be at least $100,000. Very, very, very rare. So you know, they obviously had some sort of, there are some sort of pride to that. So I kept trying to dig out of them a good offer that we could run to clients and best thing they kind of came back to me with was we'll give them a free coffee car wash. So it doesn't mean a free car wash and a coffee. That means a free coffee while you get your car washed, right? Because they got this expensive like 2,000 pound machine, this nice Italian coffee maker. And you know, I tried to run with it and you know, I split, I split test or I did a little bit of testing and just put a 20 pounds towards it. And you know, people actually took the offer. So I'm like, okay, you know what? This might actually work now. In the end, it didn't because it wasn't a strong enough poll. Now, here's the thing. If I'm going to say like a restaurant and they give me a free coffee that I'm cool with because I'm going to a restaurant either way. The thing is with a car wash and sort of car maintenance, it's not something people look forward to. So a free coffee isn't good enough incentive to get them through the door for something like that. Right? And that is the biggest thing. Whenever you're running Facebook offer ads, I really, really want you just to look, your client might try to, you know, pull you out of it. Your client might not be too comfortable with it, stuff like that. But really I want you to try and pull out the best sort of offer out of them that you possibly can because really, as I said, it's the offer that's going to get people through the door. So guys, that's, you know, that was the mistake I unfortunately made. And you know, that was a quite embarrassing one to say the least. But at least now I know what happened, which is the fact that the offer simply wasn't good enough. The offer would have been good enough. Say for example, as I said, if it was a restaurant, hey, we're going to give you a free coffee, but obviously you have to still pay for the main meal and you're still spending a lot of money when you go there because people are going to restaurants either way. They're over that first hurdle. The thing is, as I said, with a car wash, people don't enjoy getting their car washed or getting their car serviced or maintained or doing maintenance to do with our car. So, you know, that's kind of the main stumble block that came about with this ad.
